items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** f36c2590fdd199291297b197fc5b56a1040cb2e7 (PR #57773) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed The `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` library (v1.0.0-beta.1) was introduced as a new provisioning package for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, generated using the new provisioning generator. It includes resource types such as `MySqlFlexibleServer`, `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`, `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`, `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`, `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`, and `AdvancedThreatProtection`. ## Gaps Found - **Examples section is completely empty** — no code snippets demonstrating how to create a MySQL Flexible Server or related resources - **No sample test file exists** — `tests/Samples/` directory has no `.cs` files; there are no snippet-backed code examples - **"Authenticate the Client" section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication works with provisioning libraries - **"Key concepts" section contains only one generic sentence** — no description of the specific resource types available or how they relate to each other - **"Next steps" section is empty** — no links to related resources or documentation
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` (edit) Replace the empty `## Examples` section with a code snippet placeholder, and fill in the empty sections. Follow the pattern from `sdk/postgresql/Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql/README.md` as a reference. Replace the current README content with: ```markdown # Azure Provisioning MySql client library for .NET Azure.Provisioning.MySql simplifies declarative resource provisioning for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers in .NET. ## Getting started ### Install the package Install the client library for .NET with [NuGet]((www.nuget.org/redacted) ```dotnetcli dotnet add package Azure.Provisioning.MySql --prerelease ``` ### Prerequisites > You must have an [Azure subscription]((azure.microsoft.com/redacted) ### Authenticate the Client This library provisions Azure infrastructure using the Azure Developer CLI (azd) or Azure Bicep. Authentication is handled by the deployment tooling — no explicit client authentication code is required in the provisioning definition itself. ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates. The main resource types provided by this library are: - **`MySqlFlexibleServer`** — Represents an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server instance - **`MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`** — Represents a database within a flexible server - **`MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`** — Manages firewall rules to control access to the server - **`MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`** — Manages server configuration parameters - **`MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`** — Configures Azure Active Directory administrators for the server - **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackup`** / **`MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2`** — Manages server backups - **`MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`** — Manages scheduled maintenance windows - **`AdvancedThreatProtection`** — Configures Advanced Threat Protection for the server - **`MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`** — Manages private endpoint connections ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server This example demonstrates how to create a MySQL Flexible Server with administrator login credentials. ```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic ``` ## Troubleshooting - File an issue via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues). - Check [previous questions]((stackoverflow.com/redacted) or ask new ones on Stack Overflow using Azure and .NET tags. ## Next steps - Learn more about [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Explore [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) for the base provisioning library - See the [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for deployment tooling ## Contributing For details on contributing to this repository, see the [contributing guide][cg]. This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit ((cla.microsoft.com/redacted)). When you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (for example, label, comment). Follow the instructions provided by the bot. You'll only need to do this action once across all repositories using our CLA. This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][coc]. For more information, see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][coc_faq] or contact (opencode@microsoft.com) with any other questions or comments. [cg]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md [coc]: (opensource.microsoft.com/redacted) [coc_faq]: (opensource.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs` with a test demonstrating how to create a basic MySQL Flexible Server. Use `sdk/postgresql/Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql/tests/Samples/` as a reference if it exists, otherwise model after `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`. The test file should: - Be in namespace `Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests` - Use `[Test]` attribute (NUnit) - Wrap the snippet content with `#region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` / `#endregion` - Demonstrate creating an `Infrastructure`, adding parameters (admin login, admin password), creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer` with `MySqlFlexibleServerSku`, `MySqlFlexibleServerVersion`, `MySqlFlexibleServerStorage`, `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties`, and `MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability`, then adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` Example structure for `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning.Primitives; using Azure.Provisioning.MySql; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests; public class Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer { [Test] [Category("Samples")] public void CreateMySqlFlexibleServer() { #region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic Infrastructure infra = new(); ProvisioningParameter adminLogin = new(nameof(adminLogin), typeof(string)) { Description = "The administrator username of the server." }; infra.Add(adminLogin); ProvisioningParameter adminPass = new(nameof(adminPass), typeof(string)) { Description = "The administrator password of the server.", IsSecure = true }; infra.Add(adminPass); MySqlFlexibleServer server = new(nameof(server)) { Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ds_v4", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, CreateMode = MySqlFlexibleServerCreateMode.Default, Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, AdministratorLogin = adminLogin, AdministratorLoginPassword = adminPass, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled }, HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability { Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled } }; infra.Add(server); MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database = new(nameof(database)) { Parent = server, Name = "mydb" }; infra.Add(database); #endregion } } ``` **README snippet placeholder location:** In `README.md` under `### Create a MySQL Flexible Server`, after the description line, insert: ``` ```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic ``` ``` (a closing ` ``` ` follows — the `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the content) **Snippet name:** `MySqlFlexibleServerBasic` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` is currently missing or has empty content for the following sections: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ❌ Empty — add authentication guidance | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Minimal — add resource type descriptions | | Examples | ❌ Empty — add code snippet | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ Empty — add links to related resources | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags from the new test file and injects the code into `README.md` placeholder blocks. Check `git diff` after running to confirm README was updated correctly 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — only needed if any public types or members were added or changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` — ensures consistent code formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [MySql] Docs: Add examples and key concepts to Azure.Provisioning.MySql README
